(b)        fix a date for the hearing of the application for permission.


5.7       Subject to paragraph 5.8, the court officer must inform the following persons of the court’s action under paragraph 5.6—


(a)        the applicant;


(b)        P;


(c)        the person to be protected; and


(d)        any other person directed by the court.


5.8       If the application for permission was made without notice to P, paragraph 5.7(b) does not apply unless the court directs otherwise.


5.9       Where permission is granted to apply for a DAPO and—


(a)        the application for a DAPO is made on notice to P, the application must proceed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 3; or


(b)        the application for a DAPO is made without notice to P, the application must proceed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 4.


## 6. Application for a DAPO in existing proceedings


6.1       An application for a DAPO by the person to be protected made in existing proceedings under section 31(2) of the 2021 Act, in which the person and P are both parties, may be made—


(a)        in the family court or the Family Division of the High Court; and


(b)        only by the person to be protected.


6.2       The application may be made—


(a)        orally or in writing; and


(b)        without notice to P.


6.3       Subject to paragraph 6.5(a), if the application is made in writing it must be—


(a)        made using Form DA1; and


(b)        completed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 3.


6.4       If the application is made orally, the applicant must detail the information required by paragraph 3.4 to the court.


6.5       If the application is made without notice to P and—


(a)        in writing—


(i)         the applicant must explain why notice has not been given in Form DA1; and


(ii)        it must be completed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 4; or


(b)        orally, the applicant must—
